hits.title: Jayadeva (Jay) chowdappa, M.D. - Co-Founder & CEO ...
hits.snippet: The workflow didn’t. Population health is increasingly shifting from retrospective reporting to real-time engagement and coordinated outreach. Organizations are expected to demonstrate measurable improvement in quality, access, and outcomes, not just produce analytics. The difference is not better prediction. It is tighter operational loops. What operationally mature ACOs are doing differently: • Triggering outreach automatically when risk changes • Routing tasks to the right team immediately • Using multi-channel engagement to reach patients faster • Tracking intervention lag as an operational metric In many organizations, several days or even weeks pass between risk identification and first outreach.

hits.snippet: 4 days ago - Through her app, EchoHER, she creates a safe digital space for women and birthing individuals of color to share their experiences — and then uses AI (paired with human expertise) to identify themes, trends, and systemic gaps in maternal health care. And here’s the powerful part: that work led to a $326,000 investment from UnitedHealthcare to expand impact and community programming. This conversation explores: ✔️ Narrative data as evidence-based research ✔️ Innovation in Black maternal health ✔️ AI in public health equity work ✔️ Building cross-sector partnerships ✔️ Turning personal pain into population health solutions If you care about health equity, maternal mortality, digital health innovation, or public health entrepreneurship, this episode is for you.
